rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area; city center avg $3,140 is extreme — non-central furnished ~€1,800-2,000 ($1,950-2,100)

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; basic utilities 85m² avg €188 + internet ~€50

groceries.note: home cooking; Tesco/Lidl/Aldi; $324 reported monthly avg

diningOut.note: Dublin dining expensive; cheap meal ~$15, mid-range dinner for two ~$60; pubs add up

transit.note: Dublin Bus/DART monthly ~€91 Leap card; city not as transit-dense as London

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; Dublin coworking market tight — expect €250-350

simData.note: SIM-only plan (Three/Vodafone IE/48); unlimited data plans ~€20/mo

misc.note: gym (~€40), toiletries, entertainment, pub culture budget, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Dublin furnished lets are stable year-round.

evidence.quote: a mid-range lifestyle with a private studio and regular cafe visits runs 2,800-3,400 EUR ($2,910-3,540)
A 1-bedroom apartment in the city centre averages $3,140/month. Moving outside the centre saves approximately $1,652/month, bringing rent to $1,488
Basic utilities in Dublin (including electricity, heating, cooling, water, and garbage for an 85m² apartment) cost an average of £188 per month
